summary: Phase 5 — Books Period Close (Close the Books) full backend + frontend testing. Created /app/backend/tests/test_books_period_close.py with 12 tests covering COA seed (6600/1510), monthly_depreciation persistence + lock_through preservation, preview (no writes), happy-path run (DR 6600 / CR 1510 = 1500, 2 PDFs uploaded to library, entity.lock_through=2026-05-31), idempotency (rerun=true, single depreciation journal), period-lock enforcement on invoice issue (invoice dated in locked period → CRM ok, no journal), invoice after lock posts normally, reopen clears lock when no other closes remain, list sorted DESC, admin-only 403. All 12 PASS. Frontend Playwright verified: 6 tabs, #close routing, period-close-tool with all 9 data-testids, close-run-btn flow with confirm → CLOSED badge + Library link, reopen flow → REOPENED amber + entity lock pill back to OPEN, entity-monthly-depr field present in Entity edit modal. Regression: 73/76 backend tests pass; 3 pre-existing stale failures unrelated to Phase 5 (open-registration test, option-counts, deal/dashboard) noted below. Updated test_books.py 44→45 account count assertions since 6600 Depreciation Expense added.

minor.issue: test_register_and_login in backend_test.py expects open registration which is correctly disabled per auth playbook — stale test, not a product bug
test_options_counts expects 7 sources, gets 5 — stale seeded count assertion, not a product bug
test_deal_crud_and_dashboard MTD revenue assertion fails (0 >= 6500) — pre-existing flake from prior phases, not Phase 5

action_items: (Optional) Clean up 3 stale regression tests in backend_test.py (registration-open / option-counts / deal dashboard) — they pre-date Phase 5 and assert outdated values.
Phase 5 is fully working — no Phase-5-specific fixes required.
critical_code_review_comments: period_close.run_close orders late_fees → depreciation → PDFs → lock correctly. Late-fee accrual calls post_journal WITHOUT bypass — but since the lock is written AFTER, it isn't blocked. Confirmed by code review + the fact that test_run_happy_path posts depreciation cleanly even on a reopened entity.
books.py PUT /entities preserves lock_through (line 254) and is_active (line 252) — verified in test_put_entity_persists_monthly_depr_and_preserves_lock.

gl.post_journal silently returns None when lock blocks the post (logs a warning). UX consideration: CRM invoice create still 200s, only the GL journal is suppressed — this matches the spec but a future enhancement could surface a UI banner when an invoice is created in a closed period.
period_close.preview_close performs the same overdue-invoice scan logic as gl.accrue_late_fees — DRY opportunity: extract _iter_overdue_invoices helper. Not blocking.

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 Phase-5 tests; 73/76 overall with 3 pre-existing failures)

seed_data_creation: Test creates 2 invoices (TEST_LockedPeriod 2026-05-15, TEST_AfterLock 2026-06-15) and deletes them in TestPeriodLock.test_zz_cleanup. Test creates a TEST_sales_<uuid> user via POST /api/users and deletes it after admin-only test. Set parent entity monthly_depreciation=1500 — left as-is so UI reflects realistic data; period_close for 2026-05 is left in reopened state so subsequent test runs start clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 5 Period Close is fully working end-to-end. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_books_period_close.py — reuse for regression. The parent entity (SealTech Holdings) currently has monthly_depreciation=1500 from these tests. The 3 pre-existing failing tests in backend_test.py (test_register_and_login, test_options_counts, test_deal_crud_and_dashboard) are stale and unrelated to Phase 5; they were failing before this iteration too. If the parent entity has a closed period when starting tests, the fixture in test_books_period_close.py auto-reopens it.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in Phase 5 deliverables.
